GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was driven by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and core inflation, despite some moderation.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to anchor inflation expectations and support sustainable economic growth.
• Analysts suggest the central bank is balancing growth impulses with the imperative of price stability in a volatile global economic environment.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side driven inflation in India.
Evaluate the implications of a 'status quo' monetary policy on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 and comprises three members from the RBI and three external members appointed by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, EOS-07, from Sriharikota.
• Carried by the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58), the satellite is designed for high-resolution imaging and data collection.
• EOS-07 will provide crucial data for applications in agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The mission further solidifies India's indigenous capabilities in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space for national development.
• Several co-passenger payloads, including student satellites, were also deployed in the mission.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is the premier space agency of India.
PSLV is known as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites are crucial for monitoring climate change, natural resources, and environmental degradation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's national development and security.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting self-reliance and technological advancement in the space sector.
Examine the potential applications of data from EOS-07 in addressing critical challenges like food security and disaster preparedness.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with ISRO logo visible.
GS3.trivia: ISRO holds the world record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites in 2017).

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Progress in Pilot Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced significant progress in the pilot projects under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Several industrial clusters and academic institutions are demonstrating viable green hydrogen production and application technologies.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Focus areas include developing cost-effective electrolyzers, creating demand for green hydrogen in industries like fertilizers and refining, and establishing a robust regulatory framework.
• This initiative is critical for India to achieve its net-zero emissions target by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity (electrolysis).
Grey hydrogen is produced from natural gas, and blue hydrogen is produced from natural gas with carbon capture technology.
India aims to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of green hydrogen as a clean energy source for India and the challenges in its large-scale adoption.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
What policy interventions and technological advancements are required to establish India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and utilization?
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but it rarely exists freely on Earth and must be separated from other elements.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme Boosts Domestic Manufacturing of Advanced Chemistry Cell Batteries
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage received positive feedback from industry.
• Several major players have committed significant investments, leading to increased domestic production capabilities.
• This initiative is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem and renewable energy storage solutions.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependence on critical battery components and foster self-reliance.
• It is expected to create numerous jobs and establish India as a competitive manufacturing hub for ACC batteries.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme for ACC batteries was approved in 2021 with an outlay of ₹18,100 crore.
ACC batteries are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy as electrochemical or chemical energy.
The scheme aims to establish a cumulative 50 GWh of ACC manufacturing capacity in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the PLI scheme in boosting India's manufacturing capabilities and fostering self-reliance in critical sectors like ACC batteries.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for electric vehicle and battery manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of domestic ACC battery production on India's energy security and environmental sustainability goals.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of various types of batteries and electric vehicle components.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest automotive market in the world, making the transition to EVs and domestic battery production strategically important.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development Along Northern Frontiers
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated development of critical infrastructure along India's northern borders.
• Projects include new roads, bridges, and strategic tunnels to improve troop mobility and logistics in challenging terrains.
• The move is aimed at bolstering national security and responding to evolving geopolitical dynamics in the region.
• Advanced construction technologies are being deployed to overcome geographical and climatic obstacles.
• This development is part of a broader strategy to enhance connectivity and deterrence capabilities in sensitive border areas.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining road networks in India's border areas.
The northern borders include regions like Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh.
Strategic tunnels, like the Atal Tunnel, significantly reduce travel time and provide all-weather connectivity.
mainsPoints: Examine the strategic imperative behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development, particularly along its northern frontiers.
Discuss the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude and difficult terrains and the innovative solutions being deployed.
Analyze the impact of improved border infrastructure on national security, regional development, and India's geopolitical standing.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed mountain road in a snowy, high-altitude border region.
GS3.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) operates under the Ministry of Defence and has constructed over 60,000 km of roads in difficult terrains.

GS3.headline: Debate Continues Over Delimitation of Eco-Sensitive Zones in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• The long-standing debate over the demarcation of Eco-Sensitive Zones (ESZs) in the Western Ghats continues to involve state governments and environmental activists.
• Stakeholders are striving to balance conservation imperatives with the livelihood concerns of local communities.
• The recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees remain central to discussions, with states expressing reservations about their implementation.
• Environmental groups emphasize the urgent need to protect the biodiversity hotspot from unchecked development and exploitation.
• A consensus on the final boundaries and regulatory framework for ESZs is crucial for the ecological health of the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Salient features of world’s physical geography
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.
The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel - WGEEP) report was submitted in 2011.

The Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group - HLWG) report, submitted in 2013, proposed a more pragmatic approach than Gadgil, identifying a smaller area as Ecologically Sensitive Area (ESA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from developmental activities.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees regarding the conservation of the Western Ghats.
Analyze the challenges in achieving a balance between environmental conservation and the developmental aspirations of local communities in ecologically sensitive regions.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green mountains and forests of the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: MSME Sector Shows Resilience, Seeks Further Policy Support for Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector continues to demonstrate resilience but calls for enhanced policy support.
• Industry bodies highlighted challenges such as access to credit, technology adoption, and market linkages.
• Government initiatives like the 'Udyam' portal and various credit guarantee schemes have provided some relief.
• The sector remains a crucial engine for employment generation and contributes significantly to India's GDP and exports.
• Further reforms are sought to streamline regulatory processes and foster a more competitive environment for MSMEs.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
keyTerms: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are defined based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
The Udyam Registration portal was launched in 2020 for formalizing MSMEs.
The MSME sector contributes approximately 30% to India's GDP and around 45% to manufacturing output.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of the MSME sector in achieving inclusive growth and employment generation in India.
Discuss the key challenges faced by MSMEs in India and evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in addressing them.
Suggest reforms and strategies to further strengthen the MSME sector, enabling it to leverage global opportunities and contribute to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
GS3.imageDescription: A small factory building with gears and workers, symbolizing the MSME sector.
GS3.trivia: The MSME Development Act was enacted in 2006 to facilitate the promotion, development, and enhancement of the competitiveness of MSMEs.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Research and Development in Quantum Computing Technologies
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced new funding and collaborative initiatives to accelerate R&D in quantum computing.
• Focus areas include quantum hardware development, quantum algorithms, and quantum communication.
• The National Quantum Mission aims to position India as a leading nation in quantum technologies and applications.
• Collaborations between academic institutions, industry, and government labs are being prioritized to foster innovation.
• Quantum computing holds immense potential for breakthroughs in fields like drug discovery, materials science, and cryptography.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Quantum computers use principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to process information.
Unlike classical bits, which are either 0 or 1, qubits can exist in multiple states simultaneously.
The National Quantum Mission was approved in 2023 with a total outlay of ₹6,003.65 crore for 2023-24 to 2030-31.
mainsPoints: Explain the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize various sectors.
Discuss the objectives and strategic importance of India's National Quantum Mission in the global race for technological leadership.
Analyze the challenges and ethical considerations associated with the development and deployment of quantum technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum particles and connections, symbolizing quantum computing.
GS3.trivia: The term 'quantum supremacy' refers to the point where a quantum computer can perform a task that a classical supercomputer cannot perform in any feasible amount of time.

GS3.headline: Major Cities Review Urban Flooding Preparedness Amidst Climate Change Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities conducted reviews of their urban flooding preparedness plans in anticipation of changing weather patterns.
• The reviews focused on improving drainage systems, enhancing early warning mechanisms, and strengthening disaster response teams.
• Unplanned urbanization, encroachment on natural water bodies, and climate change-induced extreme rainfall are identified as key causes of urban flooding.
• Municipal corporations are collaborating with national disaster management authorities to develop resilient urban infrastructure.
• Public awareness campaigns are also being initiated to educate citizens on preventive measures and emergency protocols.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is distinct from rural flooding, primarily caused by rapid urbanization and inadequate drainage.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) provides guidelines for urban flood management.
Smart City Mission aims to promote sustainable and inclusive cities, including resilient infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of urban flooding in India and its socio-economic consequences.
Discuss the strategies and policy interventions required to enhance urban resilience to climate change-induced extreme weather events.
Evaluate the role of urban local bodies and citizen participation in effective urban flood preparedness and management.
GS3.imageDescription: A flooded city street with cars submerged in water, showing the impact of urban flooding.
GS3.trivia: Chennai, Mumbai, and Bengaluru are among the Indian cities most vulnerable to urban flooding due to their coastal location and rapid, often unplanned, urbanization.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Enhanced Maritime Security Strategy for Indian Ocean Region
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released an updated maritime security strategy, emphasizing India's role as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The strategy focuses on strengthening naval capabilities, enhancing surveillance, and fostering regional partnerships.
• It addresses emerging threats such as maritime terrorism, piracy, illegal fishing, and challenges to freedom of navigation.
• India aims to promote a rules-based order and ensure peace and stability in its strategic maritime neighborhood.
• Collaborative initiatives with littoral states for capacity building and joint exercises are central to the new approach.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
India and its neighbourhood- relations
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: The Indian Ocean Region is strategically vital, connecting major global trade routes.
India's SAGAR doctrine emphasizes security and growth for all in the region.
The Indian Navy is the primary agency responsible for India's maritime security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the geopolitical significance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and the evolving maritime security challenges it faces.
Discuss the key pillars of India's enhanced maritime security strategy and its implications for regional stability.
Evaluate the role of regional cooperation and multilateral initiatives in addressing non-traditional maritime threats in the IOR.
GS3.imageDescription: An Indian naval ship patrolling the open sea, with a map of the Indian Ocean in the background.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km and a vast Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of over 2 million sq km, making maritime security crucial.

GS3.headline: Government Promotes Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices to Boost Farmer Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched new initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture across vulnerable regions.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crops, efficient water management techniques, and agro-advisory services.
• The program aims to enhance food security, mitigate the impact of climate change on agriculture, and improve farmer livelihoods.
• Farmers are being encouraged to adopt sustainable practices like organic farming, crop diversification, and precision agriculture.
• Subsidies and technical support are being provided to facilitate the transition to climate-smart farming methods.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
keyTerms: National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA)
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts, especially in its agriculture sector.
The National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA) is a major project of ICAR.
Organic farming and Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) are practices promoted for sustainable agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and farmer livelihoods.
Analyze the various climate-resilient agriculture practices and technologies being promoted by the government.
Suggest policy measures and institutional reforms to foster widespread adoption of sustainable and climate-smart farming methods in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with advanced irrigation systems under a bright sky.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, but also one of the most climate-vulnerable.
